notes.note: A natural language name identifying the structure definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.
A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the structure definition.
The name of the organization or individual responsible for the release and ongoing maintenance of the structure definition.
The name of an individual to contact.

A copyright statement relating to the structure definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the structure definition. The short copyright declaration (e.g. (c) '2015+ xyz organization' should be sent in the copyrightLabel element.

trans-unit.source: Used by permission of HL7 Europe, all rights reserved Creative Commons License
trans-unit.target: Used by permission of HL7 Europe, all rights reserved Creative Commons License

notes.note: A name for the specification that is being mapped to.
A name for the specification that is being mapped to.
A name for the specification that is being mapped to.
A name for the specification that is being mapped to.
A name for the specification that is being mapped to.
A concise description of what this element means (e.g. for use in autogenerated summaries).

Provides a complete explanation of the meaning of the data element for human readability. For the case of elements derived from existing elements (e.g. constraints), the definition SHALL be consistent with the base definition, but convey the meaning of the element in the particular context of use of the resource. (Note: The text you are reading is specified in ElementDefinition.definition).

trans-unit.source:
Imaging Study Report composition.
A composition is a set of healthcare-related information that is assembled together into a single logical document that provides a single coherent statement of meaning, establishes its own context and that has clinical attestation with regard to who is making the statement.
While a Composition defines the structure, it does not actually contain the content: rather the full content of a document is contained in a Bundle, of which the Composition is the first resource contained.
The `text` field of each section SHALL contain a textual representation of all listed entries.
trans-unit.target:
Imaging Study Report composition.
A composition is a set of healthcare-related information that is assembled together into a single logical document that provides a single coherent statement of meaning, establishes its own context and that has clinical attestation with regard to who is making the statement.
While a Composition defines the structure, it does not actually contain the content: rather the full content of a document is contained in a Bundle, of which the Composition is the first resource contained.
The `text` field of each section SHALL contain a textual representation of all listed entries.

notes.note: A concise description of what this element means (e.g. for use in autogenerated summaries).

trans-unit.source: Organization that manages the Imaging Report
trans-unit.target: Organization that manages the Imaging Report

notes.note: A concise description of what this element means (e.g. for use in autogenerated summaries).

Provides a complete explanation of the meaning of the data element for human readability. For the case of elements derived from existing elements (e.g. constraints), the definition SHALL be consistent with the base definition, but convey the meaning of the element in the particular context of use of the resource. (Note: The text you are reading is specified in ElementDefinition.definition).

trans-unit.source: The type of imaging modality used to perform the study.
trans-unit.target: The type of imaging modality used to perform the study.

Provides a complete explanation of the meaning of the data element for human readability. trans-unit.source: The type of imaging study performed.
trans-unit.target: The type of imaging study performed.

notes.note: Text that can be used to describe the constraint in messages identifying that the constraint has been violated.

trans-unit.source: When a section is empty, the emptyReason extension SHALL be present.
trans-unit.target: When a section is empty, the emptyReason extension SHALL be present.

notes.note: A concise description of what this element means (e.g. for use in autogenerated summaries).

Provides a complete explanation of the meaning of the data element for human readability. trans-unit.source: This section holds information related to the imaging studies covered by this report.
trans-unit.target: This section holds information related to the imaging studies covered by this report.

notes.note: A concise description of what this element means (e.g. for use in autogenerated summaries).

Provides a complete explanation of the meaning of the data element for human readability. trans-unit.source: This entry holds a reference to the Imaging Study instance that is associated with this Composition.
trans-unit.target: This entry holds a reference to the Imaging Study instance that is associated with this Composition.

trans-unit.source: This section holds information related to the order for the imaging study.
trans-unit.target: This section holds information related to the order for the imaging study.

trans-unit.source: This entry holds a reference to the order for the Imaging Study and report.
